title: Subcommittee #1 (Education) Laird (D-Santa Cruz) Chair, Ochoa Bogh (R-Yucaipa), and Pérez (D-Pasadena)

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The Golden State Pathways Program is intended to promote educational pathways for high school students to high-wage, high-skill, and high-growth jobs in fields such as technology, healthcare, and education.",
    "The Department of Education did not become aware of the failure of the grant implementation until two years after the funding had been authorized.",
    "The failure of the grant implementation was described as 'a perfect storm of everything that could possibly go wrong, went wrong.'",
    "Untreated childhood trauma greatly influences youth to commit violent crimes.",
    "There is public demand for a return to reasonable public safety policies regarding youth offenders.",
    "Senator Grove proposed better state tracking of county spending and detailed reporting of program outcomes and recidivism rates.",
    "The Governor requested $5 million for a 'Belonging Campaign' to define loneliness.",
    "Research is necessary to define 'loneliness' and performance and outcome metrics will be considered as part of that endeavor.",
    "The California High-Speed Rail Project cost has increased by $95 billion from the initial promise of $33 billion.",
    "The California High-Speed Rail Project is now $100 billion short of enough money to complete construction.",
    "Operations for the California High-Speed Rail Project planned to begin in 2020 are unlikely to start before 2034.",
    "The initial trains of the California High-Speed Rail Project will only serve the Central Valley.",
    "No private investment has materialized for the California High-Speed Rail Project."
  ]
}
```
